rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KLAUS BIESENBACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: NOTICE OF HEARING FOR PETITION FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, WRIT OF MANDATE, IN CAMERA REVIEW, AND COURT-MONITORED COMPLIANCE
rulings.Rulings: 2 - NOTICE OF HEARING FOR PETITION FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, WRIT OF MANDATE, IN CAMERA REVIEW, AND COURT-MONITORED COMPLIANCE Set for Law and Motion on Friday, January 17, 2025 Line 1. Plaintiff/Petitioner Klaus Biesenbach's Motion for Hearing for Petition For Injunctive Relief, Writ Of Mandate, In Camera Review, And Court-Monitored Compliance is DENIED AS MOOT. As discussed in the order sustaining the demurrer, there are currently no claims upon which relief can be granted before the court in this proceeding. Should viable claims be made in the future, this order is without prejudice to a similar motion on those claims. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCESS ABROGAR VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF PRINCESS ABROGAR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Set for Law and Motion on Friday, January 17, 2025 Line 6 , Plaintiff Princess Abrogar's Motion For Sanctions is DENIED. The record on this motion does not establish Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Lars Nelson filed and litigated their Motion for Summary Judgment or, in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication for an improper purpose, such as to harass Plaintiff, meriting sanctions. (See CCP section 128.7(a)&(b).) Nor does the record establish Defendants acted in bad faith or filed a frivolous motion or a motion that was solely intended to cause unnecessary delay, such that sanctions must or should be imposed. (See CCP section 128.5(a)&(b).) The record on this motion does not support imposition of monetary sanctions on Plaintiff based on her "ongoing tactics in this matter." For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE KENDRICK VS. CHAPARRAL COUNTRY CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendant Chaparral Country Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE KENDRICK's Motion To Strike Defendant Chaparral Country Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication. Set for Law and Motion on Friday, January 17, 2025 Line 7 , Plaintiff Christine Kendrick's Motion to Strike Defendant Chaparral Country Corporation's Motion for Summary Judgment and Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. The court construes the motion as a common law motion to strike. It is undisputed Defendant Chaparral Country Corporation did not file its motion at least 75 days before the date for which it is noticed. The motion, thus, was not timely filed. (See Cuff v. Grossmont Union High Sch. Dist. (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 582, 596 [motion for summary judgment must be filed at least 75 days before the time appointed for hearing].) Further, the motion was not timely served. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 437(a)(2) [notice of the motion and supporting papers must be served 75 days before the hearing]; McMahon v. Superior Court (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112, 118 ["in light of the express statutory language, trial courts do not have authority to shorten the minimum notice period for summary judgment hearings"].) There is no evidence Defendant has yet served the motion it intends to have heard on January 22, 2025, and service at this time would be untimely. The court finds irrelevant Defendant's service of a prior, un-file motion and one or more errata. The parties and the court must be able to work from identical papers, which is not possible here. Even assuming the court could deem service of the un-filed motion and errata amounted to service of the complaint, that service was not complete 75 days before the noticed hearing. The court finds Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5 inapt. This is not a motion to strike a pleading as that term is defined in section 435. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: JOSH ANDERSON VS. AMY BEASOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: 1 - PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON's MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES Set for Discovery on Friday, January 17, 2025 Line 5 , Moving party shall provide a proposed order on EACH motion. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories is GRANTED. The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant has not established that it has provided any responses to the form interrogatories. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for Form Interrogatories when no responses have been provided. (CCP Section 2030.290.) Defendant shall serve verified, objection-free, substantive responses by February 18, 2025. Defendant did not act with substantial justification in connection with these discovery requests. Defendant and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $1,250 to Plaintiff in sanctions by February 18, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Evidence; Declaration Of Plaintiff, Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Evidence; Declaration Of Plaintiff, Proof Of Service Set for Law and Motion on Friday, January 17, 2025 Line 12 , The motion is denied. First, plaintiff failed to meet and confer regarding the discovery request, which is mandatory. (Townsend v. Super. Ct. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1431, 1435 ["The Discovery Act requires that, prior to the initiation of a motion to compel, the moving party declare that he or she has made a serious attempt to obtain 'an informal resolution of each issue.'"].) Second, the motion does not "set forth specific facts showing good cause for the discovery sought by the demand." (Code Civ. Proc., 2031.310, subd. (b)(1).) The court agrees that plaintiff's First Amended Complaint does not set forth a viable claim and therefore, the court cannot discern whether the discovery request is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Superior Ct. (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 216, 223-224.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
